must_have_breakdown.skill: Salesforce Platform Management or Salesforce Architecture
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Candidate led 'end-to-end delivery for Salesforce org with 1500+ users across Sales & Service Cloud' and designed scalable Apex frameworks and data models.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Expert-level proficiency with Copado for CI/CD and release orchestration
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume mentions 'using Copado' for deployment in the IBM/Informa role and holds 'Copado Fundamentals I & II' certifications, but no evidence of expert-level orchestration, pipeline architecture, or advanced Copado features.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Deep understanding of Salesforce multi-tenant architecture
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Candidate demonstrates governor-limit compliance, LDV optimization, sharing architecture, and org-wide design — all indicative of multi-tenant architecture expertise.
Resume explicitly states 'scalable Apex frameworks with strict governor-limit compliance' and 'LDV, indexing, query optimization' skills.

Candidate 'Acted as production owner, handling critical defects and release stability' but no explicit mention of managing sandbox environments, scratch orgs, or environment strategy at the platform level.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Communication skills to translate technical releases into business-friendly roadmaps
Experience managing Business Critical production environments
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Candidate 'Acted as production owner, handling critical defects and release stability' for a 1500+ user org, but no explicit mention of 'Business Critical' tier or Salesforce Mission Critical Success engagement.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Working with Salesforce premier support tiers

must_have_breakdown.evidence: 7+ years of experience delivering Salesforce solutions across enterprise environments including IBM and TCS engagements.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Custom objects, workflows, triggers, SOQL & SOSL
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume lists 'Apex Triggers, Classes and Test Classes using SOQL/SOSL', custom objects, relationships, and workflow replacement as core activities.
Candidate 'enforced Salesforce best practices', built trigger frameworks, and applied 'design patterns' mentioned in core skills.
Candidate mentored 5 developers and led end-to-end delivery, suggesting autonomy, but no explicit statement about independent operation without supervision.

reasoning: Purna Sai Ram is a strong Salesforce Developer with 7+ years of experience demonstrating solid Apex, LWC, automation, and CI/CD skills using Copado. He meets most core technical must-haves including governor limits, multi-tenant architecture knowledge, custom objects, triggers, SOQL/SOSL, and best practices. However, critical gaps exist for this Platform Manager role: his Copado experience appears to be practitioner-level (Fundamentals I & II only) rather than the expert-level CI/CD orchestration and pipeline architecture the JD demands. There is no evidence of working with Salesforce premier support tiers, no demonstration of translating technical releases into business-friendly roadmaps, and no explicit Center of Excellence or federated development strategy experience. His role is fundamentally developer-centric with some tech lead responsibilities, whereas the JD seeks a platform governance and release management leader. He is a strong developer but has not yet made the leap to Platform Manager scope.
strengths: Strong Copado CI/CD hands-on experience: Candidate used Copado for end-to-end delivery across a 1500+ user org at IBM/Informa, and holds both Copado Fundamentals I & II certifications, demonstrating practical tool usage.

Deep Salesforce technical depth: Expertise in Apex, LWC, Triggers, SOQL/SOSL, LDV optimization, async processing, and governor-limit compliance covers the core technical requirements of the role comprehensively.

Production ownership experience: Acted as 'production owner, handling critical defects and release stability' at IBM, directly relevant to the JD requirement of managing Business Critical production environments.

Strong certification portfolio: Holds 7 Salesforce certifications including Platform Developer I, Administrator, Platform App Builder, Business Analyst, and AI Specialist, demonstrating broad platform knowledge and continuous learning.
Team leadership and mentoring: Mentored 5 developers and reduced deployment defects by 30%, indicating the leadership and quality-enforcement capacity needed to establish a Center of Excellence.
gaps: No expert-level Copado orchestration evidence: The JD requires expert-level Copado proficiency including pipeline architecture and release orchestration, but the candidate only holds Fundamentals I & II certs with no evidence of configuring pipelines, managing user stories at scale, or orchestrating multi-team deployments.

No experience with Salesforce premier or Mission Critical support tiers: The JD explicitly requires experience working with Salesforce premier support tiers, which is entirely absent from the resume — this is a distinct operational skill for enterprise platform managers.

No business communication or roadmap translation experience: The JD requires translating complex technical releases into business-friendly roadmaps for stakeholders, but the resume contains no evidence of executive communication, stakeholder management, or product roadmap ownership.

No Salesforce Data Cloud or Agentforce exposure: The JD specifically calls for supporting 'Salesforce, Agentforce, and Data Cloud' environments, and while the candidate has an AI Specialist cert, there is no hands-on Data Cloud ingestion, modeling, or activation experience mentioned.

Platform Manager scope vs. Developer scope mismatch: The JD requires architecting a federated development strategy and establishing a Center of Excellence — these are organizational governance responsibilities the candidate has not demonstrated, operating primarily as a senior developer/tech lead.

scoring_factors.reason: JD does not specify a maximum notice period, so no penalty applies.
JD work structure is not specified as In-office, so no location penalty applies.
Candidate has 7+ years of experience, well above the minimum threshold of 2.5 years (half of JD's 5-year requirement).
JD gender preference is 'Any', so no penalty applies.
JD has no college preference or exclusion specified, so no adjustment applies.
